qa_pairs.question: What steps are required to remove a shared collection in Globus?
qa_pairs.answer: To remove a shared collection in Globus, navigate to 'Collections' on the left sidebar, then click on the 'Shareable by You' tab. From there, select the title of the specific shared collection you wish to remove, click the 'Delete Collection' button on the right side of the screen, and finally, confirm the deletion by clicking the red button. Deleting a collection will not affect your files or any files others may have uploaded.

qa_pairs.question: What are the important security considerations when sharing files through Globus?
qa_pairs.answer: When sharing files via Globus, it's crucial to ensure you have permission from the data owner, verify that you are sharing with the intended recipients (using email addresses is recommended unless you have the exact Globus account name), and exercise caution if sharing with a group you don't control. If granting write access, make sure to have backups of important files not located on the shared endpoint, as users can modify or delete them. It is also highly recommended to restrict sharing to a subdirectory rather than your top-level home directory for enhanced security.

qa_pairs.question: How does a user create a new group in Globus to manage sharing permissions?
qa_pairs.answer: To create a new group in Globus, click on the 'Groups' button on the left sidebar, then select the 'Create New Group' button located in the top right corner of the screen. In the 'Create New Group' window, enter a 'Group Name' and 'Group Description', choose whether the group is visible only to members (private) or all Globus users, and then click 'Create Group' to finalize the creation.
